title: Multi-line PowerShell pasted via 'powershell -Command' / 'pwsh -c' loses newlines
description: Passing multi-line PowerShell to '-Command' via the shell concatenates the lines without statement separators, producing 'Missing argument' / 'Unexpected token' parse errors. Particularly common in agent-generated terminal commands that embed loops, foreach, or here-strings.
fix_pattern: Either (a) write the script to a temp .ps1 then invoke '-File', or (b) join the lines with ';' explicitly, or (c) base64-encode and use '-EncodedCommand'. Prefer -File for anything beyond a single statement.
first_seen_in: tests/smoke-section9-tools.ps1 (extracted from inline cmd that failed)
